steps.title: Spot the two coordinated verbs
steps.content: The sentence contains two verbs joined by “and”: “This technique emphasizes [gerund phrase] and ______ [noun phrase + infinitive].” The subject “This technique” is singular; the first verb “emphasizes” is third-person singular.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: Choice A is correct. In a compound predicate, both verbs must be conjugated the same way for the same subject. “Encourages” is the third-person singular form that parallels the first verb “emphasizes.” The other options violate agreement: “have encouraged” and “are encouraging” are plural, while “encourage” is a base form. That matches choice A — A is correct.
